headline.primary_headline: Is Your Smile Holding You Back?
headline.promise_compression: Your smile may be hurting your career without you knowing it

curiosity_architecture.incomplete_pattern: Is MY smile actually limiting my career?
curiosity_architecture.resolution_promise: Porcelain bonding can dramatically improve your smile

schema_analysis.existing_schema: Dental work means painful procedures and obvious fake teeth
schema_analysis.new_schema: Painless bonding creates natural-looking dramatic improvements
schema_analysis.bridge_mechanism: Q&A format educates + no pain emphasis + performer/model expertise

headline.vsl_adaptation: What if your smile is the one thing standing between you and your big break? For performers and models under camera scrutiny, even small dental imperfections can cost you the role...

lead.opening_verbatim: Bonding is the state-of-the-art cosmetic dental technique. It can dramatically improve the color and shape of teeth, therefore improving your smile. Below I've attempted to answer some of the most commonly asked questions about bonding and aesthetic dentistry.
lead.transition_to_body: Q. What is the process of porcelain laminate bonding?

mechanism.mechanism_description: Application of a thin, custom-fabricated porcelain veneer to the front surface of a tooth. Requires very little enamel removal, no needles in many cases, and withstands normal eating. Color doesn't change because porcelain is inert. Costs 1/3 to 1/2 of traditional caps/crowns.

proof_elements.verbatim: Many of my patients are performers and models, and quite frankly, they have been nothing less than astonished at the results
I've also been astonished since these techniques go so far beyond what my traditional dental education said was possible
Fees generally are about a third to one half of the cost of caps or crowns

qa_pairs.question: What is the process of porcelain laminate bonding?
qa_pairs.answer: Simply, it is the application of a thin, custom, fabricated veneer to the front surface of a tooth.

qa_pairs.question: Are porcelain veneers suitable for all age groups?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Patients of all ages can benefit. Especially suited for those whom complete caps or crowns may not be recommended. Used for tetracycline stains, fractures from sports, peg-shaped teeth, bridging gaps, mottled enamel, stains and discolorations. Especially suitable for models and performers under camera scrutiny.

qa_pairs.question: Does the treatment hurt?
qa_pairs.answer: No, it doesn't. Very little enamel has to be removed, no needles are required and, in some cases, no tooth structure has to be removed.

qa_pairs.question: Do the veneers feel bulky?
qa_pairs.answer: Initially slightly thick feeling. Patients adjust easily within 48 hours.

qa_pairs.question: Does it require a change of eating habits?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Withstands normal biting and chewing. Caution against bobby pins, fingernails, pencils.

qa_pairs.question: What about oral hygiene?
qa_pairs.answer: Brushed and cared for same as regular teeth. Motivation of aesthetically-pleasing teeth is a definite plus.

qa_pairs.question: If the porcelain laminates come off, will it bother me?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Tooth will be no more sensitive than before. Laminate can be replaced or repaired.

qa_pairs.question: Does the color of the laminate change?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Porcelain is inert and will not change color or permanently stain.

qa_pairs.question: Are the porcelain laminates expensive?
qa_pairs.answer: Fees generally are about a third to one half the cost of caps or crowns.

qa_pairs.question: How long will the laminates last?
qa_pairs.answer: Estimating at least 5 years of wear, personally think that is conservative.

qa_pairs.question: Do you have experience with theatre people?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Significant portion of practice is aesthetically-oriented. Many patients are performers and models, and they have been nothing less than astonished at the results. I've also been astonished since these techniques go so far beyond what my traditional dental education said was possible.
